title: Complete 11-Layer Read Stack
concept: Running the eleven context layers top-down, where the order is the content and each layer sets the conditions under which the next layer's evidence means anything
teaching_objective: Teach the trader to run all eleven layers in order before the open with one written line each (calendar/catalyst, higher-timeframe auction, session, volatility regime, intermarket tone, structural location, tape confirmation, setup quality, execution permission, trade-state, review), letting the higher layer govern context and the lower govern timing, and re-running from the broken layer down when a state changes.
junior_error: Signal shopping: treating the layers as a menu of independent signals and taking the same trade off the same visible thrust regardless of context, because the tape is the only layer his screen displays. A correct lower-layer read inside a wrong upper-layer context is not a smaller edge, it is a different trade, usually someone else's.
professional_read: The same 9:32 thrust with identical delta is initiative continuation on a clean day (no events, prior imbalance with acceptance, quiet overnight, flat ZN) and unresolved post-event repricing on a corrupted day (hot 8:30 print, overnight spike-and-retrace, doubled realized vol, ZN one-timeframing lower). Same tape, different trade, because layers 1 through 5 differ.
decision_consequence: The stack is the trade; the tape is its last sentence. A lower layer can delay a trade the upper layers permit but can never authorize one they forbid. A layer with no written output is skipped, not neutral.

required_evidence.label: All eleven layers in sequence: catalyst, HTF auction, session, volatility, intermarket, location, tape, setup, permission, trade-state, review
Order is the content: each layer sets the conditions under which the next layer's evidence means anything (top-down)
Higher layer governs context, lower layer governs timing; a lower layer can delay but never authorize
Same tape, different trade: an identical thrust is a different trade when upper layers 1 to 5 differ
Each layer carries a written one-line output; a layer with no output is skipped, not neutral

forbidden_errors.description: Signal shopping: treating the layers as a menu of independent signals and ordering from whichever one agrees with the trade already wanted.
Reading a tape signal outside its catalyst, regime, and location context (noise wearing a uniform).
Re-running the entire stack on every wiggle instead of re-running from the broken layer down.

annotations.text: The order is the content; the top governs the bottom

fixture.description: Two synthetic ES mornings with an identical 9:32 thrust (three up bars, cumulative delta near +7,000, open 5512 above prior value-area high 5506). Day one: no scheduled events, prior day imbalanced higher with acceptance, quiet overnight upper third, moderate regime, ZN flat, read as initiative continuation, prepared long on the pullback to 5508. Day two: identical thrust but a hot 8:30 release, overnight spiked down and fully retraced, realized volatility doubled, ZN one-timeframing lower, read as unresolved post-event repricing and at least as likely short-covering rotation.
fixture.notes: Scenario taken from the Complete Read Stack worked example in CH13 so the fixture can be checked against the manuscript.

acceptance_criteria: All eleven layers are drawn in their fixed top-down order, not as an unordered set.
The figure shows the higher layer governing context and the lower governing timing.
The same-tape, different-trade contrast is shown driven by the upper layers, not the tape.
Each layer is shown with a written one-line output slot, making a skipped layer visible.
Every required_evidence id is rendered and stamped (render-evidence check passes).
